PROCEDURE: All-On-6 Maxilla

Findings: 

[CONFIRM — 2026-06-14 internal inconsistencies in the original; reconcile to the actual case before signing: (1) Findings/closing summary list all six implants as Nobel Active, but the technique narrative places Biomet implants at #5 and #12. (2) Torque values differ between the summary and the narrative (e.g., #3 35 vs 50 Ncm; #8 70 vs 30 Ncm; #14 35 vs 50 Ncm). (3) #8 listed as 5.0x10 in the summary vs 5x8 in the narrative. Choose the true intraoperative values throughout.]

Maxillary all-on-6. Prosthodontist provided out of bounds guide. Tuberosity to tuberosity full thickness mucoperiosteal flap was elevated. Extraction of all remaining maxillary teeth completed. [R/L] maxillary sinus exposure after extraction of tooth [#__], no perforation noted. All implants were removed/explanted. Extraction site curetted and irrigated with sterile saline. Alveoloplasty was completed with approximately [X] mm of alveolar reduction anterior and [X] mm of alveolar reduction posterior. Six Nobel Active implants were placed in the maxilla as follows:
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm, (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])

[R/L] sinus floor exposure during placement of implant [#__], which was then covered with collagen membrane at the end of the case.
Multi-unit abutments were placed on all implants, contoured to the bone profile per the out of bounds guide. A smart denture conversion technique utilized by the prosthodontist.
[R/L] maxillary sinus covered with gel foam, incision closed primarily by 3-0 Vicryl in single interrupted fashion. Tissue around abutment trimmed by tissue punch. The Prosth team then converted and delivered immediate denture to pt, screw covered by light body PVS impression material.
10 cc of Exparel was infiltrated at the conclusion of the case.

Technique:

The patient was greeted in the operating room holding area where identification was verified and the health history was updated. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The procedure was reviewed, and the consents and surgical sites were verified. The patient was transported to the operating room and placed on the operating room table in supine position. General anesthesia was induced and a nasoendotracheal tube was placed and secured. The eyes were protected. The patient was then draped in sterile fashion for a surgical procedure of the oral cavity. A surgical time-out was completed. Moist throat pack placed. 

Bilateral Maxillary Alveoloplasty/Extraction of remaining maxillary teeth:
Attention was directed to the patient’s maxilla, where a #15 blade was used to make a crestal incision, along the palatal aspect of the alveolar ridge, from the right to left first molar regions, with distal releases raising a full thickness mucoperiosteal flap. The maxilla was skeletonized in a LeFort procedure-like fashion, identifying the bilateral piriform rims. The nasal floor was conservatively dissected bilaterally to the extent of the anterior wall of the maxillary sinus. The palatal gingiva was also reflected in a subperiosteal plane. All remaining teeth and root tips were extracted with luxators, rongeurs, and dental forceps. Sinus membrane was noted to be exposed but intact at extraction socket [#__]. The maxillary implants [#__] were removed with dental forceps and a rongeurs. The reduction guide was seated and depth of alveoloplasty marked with a sterile golf pencil. A pineapple bur under copious irrigation was then used to complete maxillary alveoloplasty in x2 quadrants ([X] mm reduction). The prosthodontic team was present and verified adequate reduction of alveolar bone. All maxillary sites were then irrigated. 

Maxillary Implants x6:
Attention was then turned to implant placement. The implant osteotomy sites were planned at positions [#__] to optimize prosthetic support and biomechanical distribution for an All-on-X maxillary fixed prosthesis. The anterior implants at sites [#__] were planned with angulation toward the piriform rim to maximize anterior-posterior spread. The posterior implants at sites [#__] were planned with angulation to maximize anterior-posterior spread and avoid the maxillary sinus. An out-of-bounds guide was used to ensure appropriate implant placement under direction of the prosthodontic team. 
Attention directed to implant sites [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. Site [#__] was angled toward the piriform and verified tactually through the piriform nasal floor dissection. A Nobel Active [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. Biomet [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. 

Attention directed to implant site [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy. The sinus was encountered during the initial osteotomies and the site was prepped just under the depth of the sinus floor. Prior to implant placement a Helitape membrane was tucked within the osteotomy and the final [diameter]x[length]mm implant was placed within the osteotomy and torqued to [X] Ncm. 

Attention directed to implant sites [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. A Nobel Active [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. Biomet [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm at site [#__]. 


Attention directed to implant site [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. The osteotomy was noted to have impinged on the nasal floor through tactile sensation through piriform. The osteotomy was then moved anteriorly and redirected posteriorly toward the piriform and underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. A Nobel Active [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. 

Cover screws were then placed over implants [#__]. Profiling of the bone around the implants was accomplished with a #4 round diamond bur. The cover screws were removed and placed on implants [#__]. Profiling was accomplished in a similar fashion. 

Multi-unit abutments were then connected to each implant and torqued to manufacturer specifications. The angled multi-unit abutments were placed and secured at 15 Ncm. The abutments were fitted to the existing maxillary prosthesis to verify passive fit and appropriate alignment. Gel foam was placed within site [#__] and a collagen membrane placed on the [right/left] nasal floor. 


Six Nobel Active implants with regular platform (RP) connections were placed as follows:
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm, (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])


Prosthesis pickup: 
The patient was then handed over to the prosthodontic team where an impression pickup was captured of the implants. The prosthesis was fabricated on the back table. See prosthodontic note for details. 

Closure:
Attention re-directed to the maxilla. All sites were then irrigated with copious sterile normal saline. A collagen membrane was placed at the [right/left] nasal floor. Gel foam was packed within extraction socket [#__] and mucosa reapproximated with 3-0 Vicryl sutures in a horizontal mattress and figure of eight style fashion. The mucosal flaps were approximated initially with 3-0 vicryl tacking interrupted sutures. At site [#__] the palatal tissue notably covered the entirety of the implant and implant uncovered using a 4.0 biopsy tissue punch. Excess gingival tissue was trimmed with a #15 blade. All tissue was noted to be able to seat passively around multiunit abutments. 

Copious irrigation of oral cavity was performed and suctioned. Exparel was administered for postop analgesia. The patient was then handed over to the prosthodontic team for delivery of the provisional prosthesis. See prosthodontic note. 

Throat pack removed. Orogastric tube passed to decompress the stomach. The face was cleaned with moist gauze. Patient emerged from anesthesia and was extubated in the operating room. The patient was then transferred to the PACU for routine postoperative recovery.

[Attending] was present throughout the duration of the procedure.
